title: Silky Adventurous Canadian Duck Stir-fry
description: A fresh, silky and adventurous Canadian dish featuring duck in a mediterranean-friendly sauce thats both light and flavorful.
instructions: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Pat dry the duck with paper towels.
Mix together olive oil, minced garlic, and Dijon mustard in a small bowl. Brush this mixture on the duck, ensuring even coverage.
Season both sides of the duck generously with salt and pepper.
Place the duck breast on a rack inside a baking dish that has been lightly greased. Bake for 25-30 minutes or until cooked through (internal temperature should reach 165°F).
Meanwhile, in a separate pan over medium heat, sauté chopped red bell pepper and green onions with olive oil until the vegetables start to soften.
Add sun-dried tomatoes and Mediterranean herbs blend to the vegetable mix. Stir well.
Remove duck from oven, let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing into thin slices.
Drizzle a bit of olive oil over the veggies if desired, then serve the duck slices on top of the veggie medley with a drizzle of lemon juice and zest.
